claim: Journey Kits is a registry for end-to-end agent workflows, packaging skills, tools, learnings, memories, services, tests, and failure examples into installable kits; similar to NPM for agents
actionable: Use Journey Kits to discover and install pre-built agent workflows for common tasks like RAG, code analysis, or domain-specific automation

claim: Kits include dependencies, models, external services, failures overcome, markdown documentation, skills, and source files; versioning prevents forced updates while notifying of improvements
actionable: When publishing agent workflows, package them as kits with full documentation, dependencies list, and known failure cases so others can install without reinventing

claim: Learnings feature allows agents to report success/failure with specific versions or models; over time, kits improve as community feedback feeds back in
actionable: Share learnings about which models/versions work best with kits; contribute feedback when you discover improvements or workarounds

claim: Shared context bindings allow team agents to access common credentials and databases without storing secrets in Journey; credentials stored in systems like 1Password
actionable: Use organization-level shared resources to bind team agents to common databases and APIs; store credentials in secure vaults, not in kits

claim: Publisher reputation scores build over time; community flagging and author scanning prevent malicious kits from entering public registry
actionable: Build trust by publishing high-quality kits; reputation scores help others identify safe, reliable workflows to adopt

claim: Agent-first design allows kits to be installed via simple prompts; no need to visit website for most features; CLI installation also available for customized setups
actionable: Install kits via agent prompts (agent-first) or CLI; avoid manual website-based configuration for repeatable deployments
